mission.title: security-compliance: FR: `loose` mode allowing Docker mediaTypes
mission.description: FR: `loose` mode allowing Docker mediaTypes . Requested by 26+ users.

steps.title: Check current security-compliance deployment
steps.description:
Verify your security-compliance version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n security-compliance -l app.kubernetes.io/name=security-compliance
helm list -n security-compliance 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working security-compliance installation.

steps.title: Review security-compliance configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant security-compliance configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n security-compliance -l app.kubernetes.io/name=security-compliance
kubectl get configmap -n security-compliance -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=security-compliance
```
Right now only OCI standard mediaTypes are allowed and anything else gets rejected such as Docker V2 Manifest and ManifestList

It would help a lot to have a loose mode where Docker V2 images are allowed to be stored inside the registry.

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n security-compliance -l app.kubernetes.io/name=security-compliance
kubectl get events -n security-compliance --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "FR: `loose` mode allowing Docker mediaTypes " is functioning correctly.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with security-compliance installed or the issue environment reproducible.
